channel.title: Whalesbook Financial News
channel.description: Latest Indian and global financial market news

item.title: Paramount Seeks FCC Approval for Foreign Funds in WBD Deal
item.description: Paramount Global has asked the U.S. Federal Communications Commission (FCC) for permission to accept significant foreign investment to help fund its proposed $110.9 billion acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD). Middle Eastern funds are set to contribute nearly $24 billion, acquiring about 38.5% of the combined company's equity. Paramount stated the Ellison family will keep voting control, emphasizing this move is necessary due to intense financial pressures and consolidation trends in the media sector.

item.title: AI Powers Korea Past UK in Global Stock Market Race
item.description: South Korea has surpassed the UK as the eighth-largest stock market, its market capitalization surging over 45% to $4.04 trillion, driven by AI champions Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix. This rally contrasts sharply with the UK's modest 3% gain. Taiwan also advanced, overtaking the UK to become the seventh-largest market, powered by TSMC. These Asian economies dominate AI hardware supply chains, attracting structural capital. However, global geopolitical tensions and differing economic fundamentals present distinct outlooks for these tech-heavy markets versus traditional European economies.

item.title: ONGC Bets on Oil Supply Crunch; Production Growth Key to Unlock Value
item.description: Oil and Natural Gas Corporation (ONGC) is set to benefit from a global oil supply deficit caused by underinvestment and strong demand. Despite outperforming recently and offering a 4.3% dividend yield, its valuation is above its historical average, though still cheaper than peers. Analysts are watching closely to see if ONGC can boost production significantly to justify a higher valuation, which remains a key execution challenge.

item.title: FIIs Sell ₹1.17 Lakh Cr in India, But Buy These 5 Stocks
item.description: Foreign institutional investors (FIIs) sold about ₹1.17 lakh crore in Indian stocks during the fourth quarter of FY26. However, they actively bought shares in five specific companies: PC Jeweller, Bajaj Consumer Care, Vishal Mega Mart, MCX, and MTAR Technologies. This selective buying indicates FIIs are focusing on companies with strong growth prospects and solid fundamentals, rather than leaving the Indian market entirely, even as overall market indexes struggled.

item.title: CarTrade Tech Stock Plunges Amid AI Threat to Transaction Pivot
item.description: CarTrade Tech's stock has fallen 45-50% from its high. Investors are concerned about its shift to a transaction-heavy model and AI's disruption of its core search business. While current financials show strong revenue growth (over 27%) and expanding margins, future growth prospects are questioned. Analysts largely rate it 'Buy' with high upside targets, but the evolving digital landscape and strategy's economic impact create investment uncertainty.

item.title: India's Labour Codes Trigger Business Cost Surge with Double Overtime
item.description: India's new labour codes, effective April 1, 2026, mandate double pay for overtime beyond 8 hours daily or 48 weekly. Businesses face significant cost hikes from revised wage rules and higher social security, posing major adaptation challenges for SMEs as India formalizes its workforce.

item.title: India Probes Malaysian Wire as Import Duties End Soon
item.description: India's Directorate General of Trade Remedies (DGTR) has started investigating aluminium wire imports from Malaysia. The probe comes as existing import duties are set to expire in September. Top Indian manufacturers, including Hindalco and Vedanta, are pushing for these duties to be extended, aiming to protect their market position and pricing power. The investigation's outcome could significantly alter competition in India's growing aluminium wire sector.

item.title: El Niño 2026 Looms: India's Rural Economy Shows Surprising Resilience
item.description: India faces rising El Niño probabilities for mid-2026, posing risks to monsoon and agriculture. However, significant buffers like diversified rural incomes, strong gold loan collateral, and a bumper Rabi crop are expected to mitigate severe impacts on agricultural GVA and rural demand. The direct transmission of weather shocks to the economy has notably weakened.

item.title: LTIMindtree Navigates AI Shift: Legacy IT Shrinks, New AI Services Grow
item.description: LTIMindtree faces a dual challenge from AI. Its traditional IT services are shrinking due to lower prices and market consolidation. Meanwhile, the new AI-native and agentic services market offers significant growth. CEO Venu Lambu stresses the need for swift changes to capture this new value. LTM is investing in talent and partnerships, like with Microsoft, and addressing 'sovereign AI.' However, market reactions and global economic pressures show this is a complex transition.

item.title: India's SMC 2025 Boosts Depositories, Redefines DP Liability
item.description: India's new Securities Markets Code 2025 (SMC) names depositories as key Market Infrastructure Institutions, broadening their duties to include investor benefits and more asset types. The code requires all securities to be held digitally, removing physical certificates. It also changes Depository Participants (DPs) from agents to members, which some analysts believe might shift who is accountable, potentially impacting investor protection despite legal safeguards.

item.title: IMF Report Criticized for Western Bias, Overlooking Global South Risks
item.description: The IMF's April 2026 World Economic Outlook is criticized for generic policy advice and a risk analysis favoring developed markets. Critics say it neglects vital threats like infrastructure vulnerability and climate change, which heavily impact lower-income countries, and overlooks real economic fallout from geopolitical events beyond market sentiment.

item.title: India Mulls Sovereign Cloud Mandate for Key Sectors Amid Geopolitical Risks
item.description: New Delhi is contemplating a sovereign cloud mandate for critical industries such as energy, telecom, and banking to counter escalating geopolitical and cybersecurity threats. This potential policy pivot, spurred by the Microsoft service suspension for Nayara Energy following EU sanctions, aims to reduce reliance on foreign cloud providers and bolster national data security. However, the nation faces a significant hurdle in developing domestic cloud infrastructure competitive with global giants.

item.title: Ruchir Sharma: India's Market Lags Peers Despite Growth Amid Geopolitical Risks
item.description: Ruchir Sharma, Chairman of Rockefeller International, will discuss global economic shifts and India's market position. His commentary comes as the West Asia conflict fuels inflation and supply chain disruptions. This contrasts sharply with India's market underperformance and rupee weakness compared to peers. Despite optimistic GDP forecasts, Sharma's analysis will examine structural issues weighing on investor sentiment and the currency, especially compared to other emerging markets in 2025. The discussion will also cover AI's disruptive impact and its investment implications.

item.title: Kashmir Rail Link Opens: Boosting Trade, Testing Indian Railways' Freight Push
item.description: The newly operational Udhampur–Srinagar–Baramulla Rail Link (USBRL) has transformed freight movement into Kashmir, with cement and apples leading inbound and outbound shipments. This infrastructure milestone, crucial for regional development and resilience against highway disruptions, now serves as a litmus test for Indian Railways' ambitious plans to increase its freight market share and compete more aggressively with road transport nationwide. The project's success also highlights evolving logistics strategies among major corporations like Amazon and Maruti Suzuki, adapting to new infrastructure realities.

item.title: UCO Bank Profit Surges 23%, Sets 1% ROA Target
item.description: UCO Bank saw a 23% year-on-year net profit jump to ₹801 crore for Q4 FY26 and aims for a 1% Return on Assets by FY27, expecting margin growth from deposit repricing. However, its FY27 credit growth forecast of 12-14% is below recent actuals over 17%. This comes as the sector faces potential Net Interest Margin cuts and new regulatory rules.
